info.title: Vercel API
info.description: Vercel combines the best developer experience with an obsessive focus on end-user performance. Our platform enables frontend teams to do their best work.

get.description: Allows to retrieve the list of projects of the authenticated user or team. The list will be paginated and the provided query parameters allow filtering the returned projects.

400.description: One of the provided values in the request query is invalid.

403.description: You do not have permission to access this resource.

parameters.description: Search projects by the name field

schema.description: Search projects by the name field

parameters.description: The Team identifier to perform the request on behalf of.
parameters.name: teamId

parameters.description: The Team slug to perform the request on behalf of.
parameters.name: slug

get.description: Get the information for a specific project by passing either the project `id` or `name` in the URL.
get.summary: Find a project by id or name

patch.description: Update the fields of a project using either its `name` or `id`.

enabledAt.description: The time the feature was enabled for this project. Note: It enables automatically with the first Deployment that outputs cronjobs.

disabledAt.description: The time the feature was disabled for this project.

deploymentId.description: The ID of the Deployment from which the definitions originated.

host.description: The hostname that should be used.

path.description: The path that should be called for the cronjob.

source.description: The origin of this definition. 'api' means created via the API. Undefined means it originated from a deployment (vercel.json).

description.description: A human-readable description of what this cron job does.

hostInferred.description: Whether the host was inferred from the production deployment URL rather than explicitly provided.

expirationDays.description: Number of days to keep non-production deployments (mostly preview deployments) before soft deletion.

expirationDaysProduction.description: Number of days to keep production deployments before soft deletion.

expirationDaysCanceled.description: Number of days to keep canceled deployments before soft deletion.

expirationDaysErrored.description: Number of days to keep errored deployments before soft deletion.

deploymentsToKeep.description: Minimum number of production deployments to keep for this project, even if they are over the production expiration limit.

deploymentExpiration.description: Retention policies for deployments. These are enforced at the project level, but we also maintain an instance of this at the team level as a default policy that gets applied to new projects.

expiresAt.description: Unix ms timestamp when the project is scheduled to expire.

lockedAt.description: Unix ms timestamp when the project was locked.

lockedBy.description: userId of the actor that triggered the lock (system or admin).

sunsetSecretId.description: This is used to identify variables that have been migrated from type secret to sensitive.

legacyValue.description: Legacy now-encryption ciphertext, present after migration swaps value/vsmValue

encryptedValue.description: Contains the `value` of the env variable, encrypted with a special key to make decryption possible in the subscriber Lambda.

internalContentHint.description: Similar to `contentHints`, but should not be exposed to the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the custom environment (format: env_*)

type.description: The type of environment (production, preview, or development)

description.description: Optional description of the environment's purpose

type.description: The type of matching to perform

pattern.description: The pattern to match against branch names

branchMatcher.description: Configuration for matching git branches to this environment

verified.description: `true` if the domain is verified for use with the project. If `false` it will not be used as an alias on this project until the challenge in `verification` is completed.

items.description: A list of verification challenges, one of which must be completed to verify the domain for use on the project. After the challenge is complete `POST /projects/:idOrName/domains/:domain/verify` to verify the domain. Possible challenges: - If `verification.type = TXT` the `verification.domain` will be checked for a TXT record matching `verification.value`.

verification.description: A list of verification challenges, one of which must be completed to verify the domain for use on the project. After the challenge is complete `POST /projects/:idOrName/domains/:domain/verify` to verify the domain. Possible challenges: - If `verification.type = TXT` the `verification.domain` will be checked for a TXT record matching `verification.value`.

items.description: List of domains associated with this environment

domains.description: List of domains associated with this environment

currentDeploymentAliases.description: List of aliases for the current deployment

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the environment was created

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the environment was last updated

items.description: Internal representation of a custom environment with all required properties

serviceType.description: Service kind (Service.type). Omitted for schemas that do not define one.

framework.description: Framework slug, when the service has one (omitted otherwise).

runtime.description: Generic runtime, e.g. 'node' | 'python' | 'go' | 'ruby' | 'rust' (Service.runtime). Omitted for static builds.

repoOwnerId.description: A new field, should be included in all new project links, is being added just in time when a deployment is created. This is needed for Protected Git scopes.
A new field, should be included in all new project links, is being added just in time when a deployment is created. This is needed for Protected Git scopes.
A new field, should be included in all new project links, is being added just in time when a deployment is created. This is needed for Protected Git scopes.

projectOwnerId.description: A new field, should be included in all new project links, is being added just in time when a deployment is created. This is needed for Protected Git scopes. This is the id of the top level group that a namespace belongs to. Gitlab supports group nesting (up to 20 levels).

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the microfrontends settings were last updated.

groupIds.description: The group IDs of microfrontends that this project belongs to. Each microfrontend project must belong to a microfrontends group that is the set of microfrontends that are used together.

enabled.description: Whether microfrontends are enabled for this project.

defaultRoute.description: A path that is used to take screenshots and as the default path in preview links when a domain for this microfrontend is shown in the UI. Includes the leading slash, e.g. `/docs`

freeProjectForLegacyLimits.description: Whether the project was part of the legacy limits for hobby and pro-trial before billing was added. This field is only set when the team is upgraded to a paid plan and we are backfilling the subscription status. We cap the subscription to 2 projects and set this field for the 3rd project. When this field is set, the project is not charged for and we do not call any billing APIs for this project.

routeObservabilityToThisProject.description: Whether observability data should be routed to this microfrontend project or a root project.

doNotRouteWithMicrofrontendsRouting.description: Whether to add microfrontends routing to aliases. This means domains in this project will route as a microfrontend.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the microfrontends settings were last updated.

groupIds.description: The group IDs of microfrontends that this project belongs to. Each microfrontend project must belong to a microfrontends group that is the set of microfrontends that are used together.

enabled.description: Whether microfrontends are enabled for this project.

defaultRoute.description: A path that is used to take screenshots and as the default path in preview links when a domain for this microfrontend is shown in the UI. Includes the leading slash, e.g. `/docs`

freeProjectForLegacyLimits.description: Whether the project was part of the legacy limits for hobby and pro-trial before billing was added. This field is only set when the team is upgraded to a paid plan and we are backfilling the subscription status. We cap the subscription to 2 projects and set this field for the 3rd project. When this field is set, the project is not charged for and we do not call any billing APIs for this project.

userId.description: The user who rolled back the project.

username.description: The username of the user who rolled back the project.

description.description: User-supplied explanation of why they rolled back the project. Limited to 250 characters.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the rollback was requested.

rollbackDescription.description: Description of why a project was rolled back, and by whom. Note that lastAliasRequest contains the from/to details of the rollback.

target.description: The environment that the release targets, currently only supports production. Adding in case we want to configure with alias groups or custom environments.

targetPercentage.description: The percentage of traffic to serve to the canary deployment (0-100)

requireApproval.description: Whether or not this stage requires manual approval to proceed

duration.description: Duration in minutes for automatic advancement to the next stage

linearShift.description: Whether to linearly shift traffic over the duration of this stage

items.description: An array of all the stages required during a deployment release. Each stage defines a target percentage and advancement rules. The final stage must always have targetPercentage: 100.

stages.description: An array of all the stages required during a deployment release. Each stage defines a target percentage and advancement rules. The final stage must always have targetPercentage: 100.

canaryResponseHeader.description: Whether the request served by a canary deployment should return a header indicating a canary was served. Defaults to `false` when omitted.

rollingRelease.description: Project-level rolling release configuration that defines how deployments should be gradually rolled out

fromRollingReleaseId.description: If rolling back from a rolling release, fromDeploymentId captures the "base" of that rolling release, and fromRollingReleaseId captures the "target" of that rolling release.

isEnvVar.description: When there was only one bypass, it was automatically set as an env var on deployments. With multiple bypasses, there is always one bypass that is selected as the default, and gets set as an env var on deployments. As this is a new field, undefined means that the bypass is the env var. If there are any automation bypasses, exactly one must be the env var.

note.description: Optional note about the bypass to be displayed in the UI

slugs.description: System environment slugs (`production`, `preview`) and/or custom environment slugs defined on the referenced project.

oneOf.description: The source envs on the trusted project that are allowed to access `to`.

slugs.description: System environment slugs (`production`, `preview`) and/or custom environment slugs defined on the referenced project.

oneOf.description: The source envs on the trusted project that are allowed to access `to`.

slugs.description: System environment slugs (`production`, `preview`) and/or custom environment slugs defined on the referenced project.

oneOf.description: The target envs on the current project that may be accessed.

slugs.description: System environment slugs (`production`, `preview`) and/or custom environment slugs defined on the referenced project.

oneOf.description: The target envs on the current project that may be accessed.

items.description: Optional overrides for the default same-env-by-slug matching. Provide explicit rules to allow cross-env access or presets.

customAllow.description: Optional overrides for the default same-env-by-slug matching. Provide explicit rules to allow cross-env access or presets.

slugs.description: System environment slugs (`production`, `preview`) and/or custom environment slugs defined on the referenced project.

oneOf.description: The target envs on the current project that may be accessed.

slugs.description: System environment slugs (`production`, `preview`) and/or custom environment slugs defined on the referenced project.

oneOf.description: The target envs on the current project that may be accessed.

onPullRequest.description: Whether the Vercel bot should comment on PRs

onCommit.description: Whether the Vercel bot should comment on commits

createDeployments.description: Whether the Vercel bot should automatically create GitHub deployments https://docs.github.com/en/rest/deployments/deployments#about-deployments NOTE: repository-dispatch events should be used instead

disableRepositoryDispatchEvents.description: Whether the Vercel bot should not automatically create GitHub repository-dispatch events on deployment events. https://vercel.com/docs/git/vercel-for-github#repository-dispatch-events - `true`: disable repository-dispatch events for this project (explicit override of the team setting). - `false`: enable repository-dispatch events for this project (explicit override of the team setting). - absent: inherit from `team.disableRepositoryDispatchEvents`.

requireVerifiedCommits.description: Whether the project requires commits to be signed & verified before deployments will be created. - `true`: require verified commits for this project (explicit override of the team setting). - `false`: do not require verified commits (explicit override of the team setting). - absent: inherit from `team.requireVerifiedCommits`.

gitCommitStatus.description: Whether Vercel should post commit statuses for this project. When omitted, commit statuses remain enabled.

enabled.description: Whether consolidated commit status is enabled.

propagateFailures.description: Whether to propagate individual deployment failures to the consolidated status.

consolidatedGitCommitStatus.description: Configuration for consolidated git commit status reporting. When enabled, Vercel will post a single consolidated commit status instead of individual statuses for each deployment.

firstEnabledAt.description: Timestamp when the feature was first enabled. Never changes after initial enablement.

pageIntegrityEnabled.description: Whether Page Integrity is enabled for this project. Used by the metadata service to gate DynamoDB lookups against the page-integrity-inventory table.

enabled.description: Whether or not to generate OpenID Connect JSON Web Tokens.

oneOf.description: Allowlist entry for GitHub and Bitbucket, whose repos are identified by a flat `org`/`repo` (Bitbucket's workspace/owner maps to `org`, its repo slug to `repo`). Omit `repo` to match any repo in the org. Org is matched case-insensitively.

Allowlist entry for GitLab, which uses nested groups rather than a flat org/repo. `namespace` is the full group path (e.g. `group` or `group/subgroup`); `project` is the leaf project name. Omit `project` to match any project under the namespace. Namespace is matched case-insensitively.

items.description: Customer-configurable deployment sources. Every deploy classifies to exactly one. JSON schema in `packages/deployment-policy/schemas/body.ts` enumerates exactly these values. - `'git'` — git provider webhook. - `'cli'` — Vercel CLI (legacy classic-token CLI and SIWV CLI both). - `'rest-api'` — direct user/team-token REST upload. Does NOT cover deploy hooks, Marketplace integrations, or first-party app tokens. - `'deploy-hook'` — project deploy-hook URL. The URL is the credential. - `'integration'` — third-party Marketplace actor: Marketplace integration token, user-delegated OAuth from a Marketplace app, or an unrecognized third-party Vercel App. First-party Vercel Apps are never `'integration'`. - `'v0'` — the v0 product surface (entitlement-gated). v0 deploys through the CLI under the hood, but classifies as its own source so a team can allow or deny v0 independently of `'cli'`. First-party Vercel apps (Toolbar, etc.) classify as `'first-party'` — see `ClassifiedSource` in `./checks`. They're not in this union because they aren't customer-configurable; they bypass `checkDeploymentSources` entirely. v0 is intentionally NOT among them: like the CLI, it's a real product surface and is policy-controllable.

deploymentPolicy.description: Project shape. `null` on a rule list clears the project's override for that rule type (fall back to team for every env); omitting is equivalent. Setting `deploymentPolicy` itself to `null` clears every override at once. Kept structurally distinct from {@link TeamDeploymentPolicy} so the two storage locations don't share a type by accident.

kind.description: Billing mode. Always 'flat' for flat-rate projects.

exceededAllowanceUntil.description: Timestamp until which the project has exceeded its CDN allowance.

bypassThrottleUntil.description: Timestamp until which throttling is bypassed (project pays list rates for overage).

throttled.description: Whether the project is currently throttled.

400.description:
One of the provided values in the request body is invalid.
One of the provided values in the request query is invalid.
Trusted IPs is only accessible for enterprise customers

402.description:
The account is missing a payment so payment method must be updated
Pro customers are allowed to deploy Serverless Functions to up to `proMaxRegions` regions, or if the project was created before the limit was introduced.
Deploying to Serverless Functions to multiple regions requires a plan update

403.description: You do not have permission to access this resource.

409.description:
The provided name for the project is already being used
The project is currently being transferred.

428.description:
Owner does not have protection add-on
Advanced Deployment Protection is not available for the user plan

buildCommand.description: The build command for this project. When `null` is used this value will be automatically detected

customerSupportCodeVisibility.description: Specifies whether customer support can see git source for a deployment

devCommand.description: The dev command for this project. When `null` is used this value will be automatically detected

framework.description: The framework that is being used for this project. When `null` is used no framework is selected

gitForkProtection.description: Specifies whether PRs from Git forks should require a team member's authorization before it can be deployed

gitLFS.description: Specifies whether Git LFS is enabled for this project.

protectedSourcemaps.description: Specifies whether sourcemaps are protected and require authentication to access.

installCommand.description: The install command for this project. When `null` is used this value will be automatically detected

name.description: The desired name for the project

outputDirectory.description: The output directory of the project. When `null` is used this value will be automatically detected

previewDeploymentsDisabled.description: Specifies whether preview deployments are disabled for this project.

previewDeploymentSuffix.description: Custom domain suffix for preview deployments. Takes precedence over team-level suffix. Must be a domain owned by the team.

publicSource.description: Specifies whether the source code and logs of the deployments for this project should be public or not

functionDefaultRegions.description: The regions to deploy Vercel Functions to for this project

functionZeroConfigFailover.description: Specifies whether Zero Config Failover is enabled for this project.

resourceConfig.description: Specifies resource override configuration for the project

rootDirectory.description: The name of a directory or relative path to the source code of your project. When `null` is used it will default to the project root

serverlessFunctionRegion.description: The region to deploy Serverless Functions in this project

serverlessFunctionZeroConfigFailover.description: Specifies whether Zero Config Failover is enabled for this project.

skewProtectionBoundaryAt.description: Deployments created before this absolute datetime have Skew Protection disabled. Value is in milliseconds since epoch to match \"createdAt\" fields.

skewProtectionMaxAge.description: Deployments created before this rolling window have Skew Protection disabled. Value is in seconds to match \"revalidate\" fields.

skewProtectionAllowedDomains.description: Cross-site domains allowed to fetch skew-protected assets (hostnames, optionally with leading wildcard like *.example.com).

skipGitConnectDuringLink.description: Opts-out of the message prompting a CLI user to connect a Git repository in `vercel link`.

sourceFilesOutsideRootDirectory.description: Indicates if there are source files outside of the root directory

enablePreviewFeedback.description: Opt-in to preview toolbar on the project level

enableProductionFeedback.description: Opt-in to production toolbar on the project level

enableAffectedProjectsDeployments.description: Opt-in to skip deployments when there are no changes to the root directory and its dependencies

enableExternalRewriteCaching.description: Specifies whether external rewrite caching is enabled for this project.

staticIps.description: Manage Static IPs for this project

enabled.description: Opt-in to Static IPs for this project

domains.description: Comma-separated list of drain endpoint domains

requestPath.description: Request path prefix to apply the sampling rule to

oidcTokenConfig.description: OpenID Connect JSON Web Token generation configuration.

enabled.description: Whether or not to generate OpenID Connect JSON Web Tokens.

passwordProtection.description: Allows to protect project deployments with a password

deploymentType.description: Specify if the password will apply to every Deployment Target or just Preview

password.description: The password that will be used to protect Project Deployments

ssoProtection.description: Ensures visitors to your Preview Deployments are logged into Vercel and have a minimum of Viewer access on your team

deploymentType.description: Specify if the Vercel Authentication (SSO Protection) will apply to every Deployment Target or just Preview

trustedIps.description: Restricts access to deployments based on the incoming request IP address

deploymentType.description: Specify if the Trusted IPs will apply to every Deployment Target or just Preview

value.description: The IP addresses that are allowlisted. Supports IPv4 addresses and CIDR notations. IPv6 is not supported

note.description: An optional note explaining what the IP address or subnet is used for

protectionMode.description: exclusive: ip match is enough to bypass deployment protection (regardless of other settings). additional: ip must match + any other protection should be also provided (password, vercel auth, shareable link, automation bypass header, automation bypass query param)

label.description: The label or description of the trusted source

to.description: A set of environments, expressed as explicit slugs, a named preset, or both. At least one of `slugs` or `preset` must be set.

items.description: A system environment (\"production\", \"preview\", or \"development\") or a custom environment slug

from.description: A set of environments, expressed as explicit slugs, a named preset, or both. At least one of `slugs` or `preset` must be set.

items.description: A system environment (\"production\", \"preview\", or \"development\") or a custom environment slug

customAllow.description: Optional overrides for the default same-env-by-slug matching.

label.description: The label or description of the trusted source

to.description: A set of environments, expressed as explicit slugs, a named preset, or both. At least one of `slugs` or `preset` must be set.

items.description: A system environment (\"production\", \"preview\", or \"development\") or a custom environment slug

anyOf.description: Composable deployment-time policy. Each rule type holds a list of rules, one per environment scope.

optionsAllowlist.description: Specify a list of paths that should not be protected by Deployment Protection to enable Cors preflight requests

value.description: The regex path that should not be protected by Deployment Protection

connectConfigurations.description: The list of connections from project environment to Secure Compute network

connectConfigurationId.description: The ID of the Secure Compute network

passive.description: Whether the configuration should be passive, meaning builds will not run there and only passive Serverless Functions will be deployed

buildsEnabled.description: Flag saying if project builds should use Secure Compute

dismissedToasts.description: An array of objects representing a Dismissed Toast in regards to a Project. Objects are either merged with existing toasts (on key match), or added to the `dimissedToasts` array.`

key.description: unique identifier for the dismissed toast

dismissedAt.description: unix timestamp representing the time the toast was dimissed

action.description: Whether the toast was dismissed, the action was accepted, or the dismissal with this key should be removed

post.description: Create one or more environment variables for a project by passing its `key`, `value`, `type` and `target` and by specifying the project by either passing the project `id` or `name` in the URL. If you include `upsert=true` as a query parameter, a new environment variable will not be created if it already exists but, the existing variable's value will be updated.
post.summary: Create one or more environment variables
